packets.brief_html: This message should be used for manipulating the trading window, the Horadric Cube item window, and the Stash window.

This will request 16 ladder entries, starting at a zero-based location specified in 'Starting position'. For example if this is 0, then ladder entries 1-16 are retrieved. If this is 17, then ladder entries 18-33 are retrieved. Note: The values that Diablo 2 sends for this are always perfectly divisible by 16. This might be a requirement.
Client should set the internal Battle.net server list from the contents of this message.
This packet contains news from Battle.net. Timestamps are in C/Unix format, biased for UTC.
Battle.net requests optional work from the Client using this message. See ExtraWork document for more details.
Converts a non-expansion character to expansion.
Success is TRUE if the server knows your password, FALSE otherwise. If this is FALSE, the Battle.net connection should be closed by the client.
Requests Battle.net to change the email address bound to an account. This packet must be sent before logon.
Reports success or failure on the game key and version check.
Received when attempting to join a closed Battle.net game using MCP_JOINGAME.
This message is used to change the client's password.
Returns the requested registry value from S>C 0x18 SID_REGISTRY.
Command to all bots.
Uses the currently selected skill at the specified location.
Contains extended profile information as requested by the client.
This message will confirm that the server really knows your password.
Replaces item in belt with item in player's cursor buffer.
This message will give you data you need for SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCHANGE (0x55).
Notifies online members that a member has changed rank or online status.
Sent to clients supporting this packet when a friend is promoted up or demoted down the friend's list.
Sent to clients supporting this packet when an account is added to the client's friendlist. New friends are appended to the list.
Requests a character list.
Returns filename and filetime of default icons file. This information can be used to download the file from BNFTP and information on parsing it can be found be found here.

Requests the filename & time of the default icons file for the current game. This message must be sent after client authentication and before receiving SID_ENTERCHAT or Battle.net will terminate the connection.

Updates the Client's profile.
Currently, the allowed keys for this are Profile\Sex, Profile\Location, and Profile\Description. The maximum length for the values is 512, including the null terminator.
Contains encryption result and data to be sent to Battle.net.
Receive cycle request (defunct).
Swaps item in player's cursor buffer with item in the body location.
Provides values used with CheckRevision().
Walk to a specified (X,Y) map coordinate.
Disbands the clan.
Creates a character on the Realm.
Character Class Id:
This message will give you data you need for SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCHANGE (0x55). This message is used to change the password of an existing account.

Swaps the item in the player's cursor buffer with one in the player's inventory. Please note:
This message's official name is not known, and has been invented.
On failure, Product is 0. On success, this is equal to the requested Product.
The clients send this when an ad is clicked.
Pick up a ground item to cursor buffer/inventory.
Drops a pile of gold to the ground.
The message reports the success or failure of an account creation attempt.
Removes a member from the clan.
Receive command over BotNet.
Requests the URL for an ad if none is given.
A character is trading with you.
Sent when an ad is displayed. Perhaps for statistics?
Realm password is always "password".
Joins Chat.
Returns server parameters and a versioning challenge.
Server-assigned coordinate, object ID, and state/interaction properties.
Begins advertising your game to Battle.net so others may find it.
Received to declare that the client is a member of a clan.

Uses your right skill repeatedly on the Entity specefied in Entity ID.
This packet is sent repeatedly when the mouse button is held down after the initial packet has been sent.
Requests a list of candidates to create a clan.

This message will parse data from SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTLOGON (0x53) and will reply with data to send in SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTLOGONPROOF (0x54). You must send BNLS_LOGONCHALLENGE (0x02) before you can send this.

This packet contains the current user's Friends List. If the friend is offline, their Product id is 0x00. Location is only supplied when it is relevant; for example, if Status is set to offline (0x00), the location string will be empty.
Logs onto the realm.

This message will request a fast version check. Now works with all products.
Version DLL Digit must be in the range 0-7. (For example, for IX86Ver1.mpq this is 1)
Uses your left skill on the Entity specified in Entity ID.
Received when a user is inviting you to create a new clan.
Result of a member information request.
Pickup an item from the possible buffers below, moving it to the cursor buffer.
Requests information about a member of a clan.
Contains the server token.
Information about a specific game. The game client requests this when a game is selected in the list.
Creates a game in a manner similar to SID_STARTADVEX and SID_STARTADVEX3.
Used to calculate Client's ping. The received UINT32 should be sent directly back to Battle.net.
This packet contains the UDP code to be sent to Battle.net in SID_UDPPINGRESPONSE (0x14).
Contains system information.
Sets the message of the day.
Stops interacting with an NPC
Places a scroll into a tome of scrolls.
Contains unhashed CD key information.
Update client information.
Requests the realm's MOTD.
Possible Action IDs:
Keeps the connection alive. This message should be sent to the server every 8 minutes (approximately).
Requests information about a game.

After this message is received, the logon checking sequence for a particular logon session is complete. This message can also be used to calculate the server's SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCHANGEPROOF (0x56) response, and check the client's change password request. Simply substitute the SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTLOGONPROOF (0x54) data with the SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCHANGEPROOF (0x56) data.
This is sent to create a closed Battle.net game.
Contains the result of a game list status update.

This message will encrypt your CD-key, and will reply with the properly encoded CD-key as it is supposed to be sent in the message C>S [0x51] SID_AUTH_CHECK. It now works with CD-keys of all products.

This message requests the latest version byte for a given product.
The version byte is sent to Battle.net in SID_AUTH_INFO (0x50).
Battle.net requests required work from the Client using this message. See ExtraWork document for more details.
This message will give you data you need for SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCREATE (0x52).
This packet is identical to SID_CDKEY (0x30)
This is sent to the server when receiving the status code in BNLS_AUTHORIZE (0x0E).

Contains Client product, realm, statstring, and is sent as the response when the client sends SID_ENTERCHAT. Unique name is the users unique name in chat (Which may be Arta, Arta#2, Arta#3, etc). Account name is the users account name (Which in all 3 previous examples would be Arta).
Contains end-of-game statistics, sent to the server by all clients that participated in the game.
This packet is identical to SID_CDKEY2 (0x36)
Lists characters.
Uses your right skill on the Entity specefied in Entity ID
Requests a friends list from Battle.net.
Uses your left skill on the Entity specefied in Entity ID.
Changes the rank of a member of the clan.
Notifies Battle.net that the client has joined a game. This is what causes you to receive "Your friend entered a game called _." from Battle.net if you are mutual friends with this client.
Contains the latest filetime for the requested file. If the file does not exist, the returned filetime is null.
If the client sent a valid account name, but the password checksum is incorrect, the connection is terminated. Otherwise, this response is sent.
Acknowledge chat options.
User information updated.

This packet is sent to establish that the client supports UDP. In particular, when the client is using a non-standard port (e.g. not port 6112), this message is used to tell the server where it should send PKT_CONNTEST2 messages.

This message is sent to upgrade an XSHA-1 account to an NLS/SRP-style account. It should be sent when S>C 0x53 SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTLOGON or S>C 0x55 SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCHANGE indicates that an account upgrade is required.
This message contains the data to send in SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTUPGRADEPROOF (0x58).
Specifies host & port that a game creator is using for a game.
This message contains the data to send in SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCHANGEPROOF (0x56).
Acknowledge identifying yourself with BotNet.
This message will parse data from SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTUPGRADE (0x57) and will reply with data to send in SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTUPGRADEPROOF (0x58).

Initiate an NPC sesstion, sent following:
C->S 0x13
It indicates that you are now interacting with an NPC, and a dialog window is opened.
Receive chat.
If the Success code is TRUE, the revision number was recognized by the server and will be used. If it is FALSE, the revision number was rejected by the server and this request is ignored.
Log on to, create, or modify account.
Reports the success or failure of the logon request.
Result of inviting a user to join your clan.
All phrases sent to the server will be heard by all players in your vicinity.
This message contains the information required for the specified product.
This message will request the data you need for SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCREATE (0x52).
Buys an item from a Non Player Character.
Contains the members of a clan.
Sent to accept or decline an invitation to a clan.
Provides password proof when upgrading the account from XSHA-1 logon system to NLS/SRP logon system. This message is no longer used.
Reports success/failure if the account password was changed or not. This is used for the old logon system.
Sets the current (base) amount of the specified attribute.
Sets the current (base) amount of the specified attribute.
Requests the client to bind an email address to the account.
This is sent to begin joining a game from the game list.
Contains Client's username and hashed password. This message is the same as C>S 0x29 0xSID_LOGONRESPONSE, but has additional response codes.
Creates a Battle.net account. Usernames longer than 15 characters are truncated.
Joins a channel after entering chat.
Reports success/failure on account creation, sent in response to C>S 0x2A SID_CREATEACCOUNT.
User disconnected.
A character moves to an object within your view range.
Identify yourself with BotNet.
Run to a specified (X,Y) map coordinate.
Requests a user's status on ladder.

This packet is sent to establish that the client supports UDP. In particular, when the client is using a non-standard port (e.g. not port 6112), this message is used to tell the server where it should send PKT_CONNTEST messages.
Received when a user is inviting you to join their clan.
Lists characters.

This packet supplies the data necessary to connect to an MCP server. The cookie value is defined in the first UINT32 of SID_LOGONREALMEX and is simply returned by Battle.net. If the length of the message (not including header) is greater than 8, the client should continue to logon to the MCP. Otherwise, the Status field is interpreted as an error code:
If Success is TRUE, the signature matches the server's IP. If Success is FALSE, the signature does not match.

This message reserves a number of slots for concurrent NLS checking operations. No other NLS checking messages can be sent before this message has been sent.This message cannot be sent more than once per connection.
Response for both S>C 0x4A SID_OPTIONALWORK and S>C 0x4C SID_REQUIREDWORK. See ExtraWork document for more details.

Returns a list of available players for an arranged team ladder game. Players who are considered available: have no away message on, are mutual friends in the chat environment, or non-friend listed users in the same channel as you.
Sent prior to a disconnect along with SID_MESSAGEBOX to indicate that the client has flooded off.
Database data updated.
This packet was used to ensure that the client's account number was valid. All but the last two fields in this message are now ignored, and may be set to zero.

The compression mode is one of:
0x81 : custom compression mode, data follows (unused)
0x01 : compression enabled, use default compression
0x00 : no compression (unused)
The return value for character creation.
Requests news and MOTD from battle.net.
Response to a member rank change request.
Result of profile lookup for WarCraft III.
Requests ad banner information from Battle.net.
This message verifies a server's signature, which is based on the server's IP. The signature is optionally (currently sent only with Warcraft 3) sent in SID_AUTH_INFO (0x50).

This message performs two operations. First, it checks if the client's logon was successful. Second, it calculates the data for the server's reply to SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTLOGONPROOF (0x54). If this data is not correct, then the client will not accept the logon attempt as valid.
This message requests an update on a specific user on the friends list. Battle.net should reply immediately with the same packet (see related).
Contains the requested ladder data.
Account creation result.

Sent to clients supporting this packet when an entry is removed from the client's friendlist. The first friend is Entry number 0. Accounts positioned after the specified Entry number in the friendlist are moved up by one.
Changes the clan's chieftain.
Moves item from body location to player's cursor buffer.
Invites the specified users to create a new clan.
This message will request a fast version check and parses the version check filename so the client does not have to. Now works with all products.
Requests a character list.
Contains Client's username and hashed password.
Command to bots on your database.
This message is originally received compressed, so the message ID will correspond with D2GS compressed format.
Used by clients to inform the server that a game has been created, or that the state of a created game has changed.
Returns a list of available games and their information. Values vary depending on product.

Unselects the selected object. Please note: This message's official name is not known, and has been invented. The contents of this packet may be incomplete or incorrect, and your comments and contributions are requested.
Ping response. Ping Value is the UINT32 received in the server's initial ping message.
Sent when you pick up a UINT8 (255) of gold(254 or less).
This message is sent after receiving a successful SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCHANGE message, and contains the proof for the client's new password.
This message is sent to inform the server that a game should no longer be advertised to other users. It is sent when a game starts, or when a game is aborted (the host leaves).
Contains information needed to download and display an ad banner.

This packet is part of the logon sequence, not to be confused with the other 0x5C.
This packet is originally received compressed, so the message ID will correspond with [Protocol Headers] D2GS compressed format.
This message is received with the 'OK' that you can go ahead and enter the gaming environment.
This message is sent to create an NLS-style account. It contains the client's salt and verifier values, which are saved by the server for use with future logons.
Drops the item in the player's cursor buffer to the ground.
Moves the specified item from the belt to the player's cursor buffer.
Realm list.
Contains the list of potential candidates.
Sends information about the client's product and locale to Battle.net.
Verifies that a file is authentic, by producing a hash of that file and sending it to the server for comparison to the original.
Requests to go to a waypoint if it was already activated.
Uses the currently selected skill at the specified location.
This message instructs the server which revision of NLS you want to use.
Moves an item into the player's belt.
Result of inviting users to create a clan.
Informs the client of the UDP Token and the Server Token.
Informs the server of the client's locale information. Much of this functionality has been incorporated into C>S 0x50 SID_AUTH_INFO, and more in-depth remarks can be found there.
Invites the specified user to your clan.
Requests the list of clan members.
Reports the Adbanner's URL. This message will only be received if the client sent it to the server.
Creates a game in a manner similar to SID_STARTADVEX2 and SID_STARTADVEX3.
This message is still being researched!

This message will calculate the hash of the given data.
The hashing algorithm used is the Battle.net standard hashing algorithm also known as "Broken SHA-1"
Keep the connection alive.
Requests a game listing.
Uses Left skill on specified (X,Y) map coordinate.
Notifies online members that a member has left or has been removed.
Requests ladder listing.
Updates an attribute and sets it to the value sent, 4 bytes max.
This byte should be sent in order to start receiving in-game messages and to interact with the world itself.
Response to a clan change chieftain request.
This message is used when you'd like to put a message above a character's head as used in the client as "![message]".
Notifies you when you have left or are removed from a clan.
Creates an account.
This message will give you data you need for SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTLOGON (0x53). You must send this before you can send BNLS_LOGONPROOF (0x03).
Contains all chat events.
Position in line to create a game.
Acknowledge identifying yourself with BotNet.
Upgrades the account from XSHA-1 logon system to NLS/SRP logon system. This message is no longer used.
Keep the connection alive.
Send chat.
This message requests a list of players for an arranged team game.
Reports success/failure on password authentication.

This message initializes a new logon checking session and calculates the values needed for the server's reply to SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTLOGON (0x53).
BNLS_RESERVESERVERSLOTS(0x12) must be sent before this message to reserve slots for logon checking sessions.
Notifies the server that you want to cancel the creation of your game.
Moves item from player's cursor buffer to body location. Body locations are identified by the line number in the data file bodylocs.txt.
Result of file integrity query.
Sent to accept or decline an invitation to create a clan.

This packet authenticates the client with the MCP and allows character querying and logon to proceed.
All 16 UINT32s (Cookie, Status, Chunk 1, and Chunk 2) are received from the server via SID_LOGONREALMEX.
Contains the current message of the day.
Requests a realm listing.
This message is sent to the server to initiate a logon. It consists of the client's public key and their UserName.
Realm list.
Contains the realm's MOTD. The Byte at the beginning is apparently ignored.
This message will parse data from SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCHANGE (0x55) and will reply with data to send in SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTCHANGEPROOF (0x56).

Interacts with the specified Entity.
For players and npc's, it will send a request to interact.
The interaction depends on the type of the unit or object.
For others it will trigger the object, for example using a shrine, looting a corpse you have permission to loot, or opening and closing a door.
This message is used to determine the latency to a game on Battle.net.
Contains data for SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTLOGON (0x53).
This message confirms the validity of the client password proof and supplies the server password proof. See NLS/SRP Protocol for more information.
Contains CheckRevision values.
Binds an email address to your account.
Deletes a character.
Requests a realm listing.
Set chat options.
Uses the specified item (such as a potion, town portal scroll, etc.).
Logon response.
Changes Battle.net account password. This message must be sent before you logon.
Leaves chat mode but does not disconnect. Generally sent when entering a game. This is also sent by D2DV/D2XP when switching characters, and by all products when logging off.
Notifies Battle.net that you have left a Diablo II game.
This message was used to check a digest of a game file. This message is no longer used; developers should use the SID_CHECKDATAFILE2 message.
Converts a non-expansion character to expansion.

The Cookie has no special meaning to the server and will simply be echoed to the client in the response. The client can use multiple types of CD-keys in the same packet. The number of CD Keys to encrypt should be between 1 and 32.
This message was historically used to issue new values to the client for the above fields. New values were issued when the values supplied in SID_CLIENTID were found to be invalid.
Inserts the specified item into a socketed item.

Displays a message to the user. This message's fields are used as parameters for the Win32 API MessageBox, and can be passed directly to it. For more information about these parameters, see the documentation for MessageBox at MSDN.
Contains CheckRevision response, version & EXE info.
If the success code is TRUE, you may send SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTUPGRADE (0x57).
Sets the port used by the client for hosting WAR3/W3XP games. This value is retrieved from HKCU\Software\Blizzard Entertainment\Warcraft III\Gameplay\netgameport, and is sent after the user logs on.
Data for SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTLOGONPROOF (0x54).
The result of attempting to create a closed Battle.net game.
This packet should be sent every five to seven seconds to avoid timeout.
Moves item from the player's cursor buffer to an inventory space.
Received when requesting ladder data.
This may be equal to the number of slots requested, although it does not necessarily have to be the same value.
This message is empty and may be used to keep the connection alive. The client is not required to send this. There is no response from the server.
Command to specific bot.
Requests an extended profile.
This packet is an updated version of SID_CDKEY (0x30), designed to prevent CDKeys from being stolen, by sending them hashed instead of plain-text.
Send text or a command to Battle.net using this packet.
This message will request a fast version check. Now works with all products.
When the flags do not contain CDKEY_OLD_STYLE_RESPONSES (0x08), the response is this message.
Contains a list of available channels, each terminated by a null byte. The last item should be empty (just a null byte), indicating the end of the list.
Stacks one item such as a key onto another item.
Makes your character walk to the Entity specified in Entity ID.
Log on to, create, or modify account.

Instead of receiving a single response that has a list of all the games, the client will receive this packet once for every game listed by the server. The list ends when the game name is an empty string.
Request the user list from BotNet.
Keeps the connection alive. Clients do not need to respond to this message.
Takes the potion your cursor holds and gives it to the mercenary. Please note: This message's official name is not known, and has been invented.

This message is sent to the server to start the process of checking the game files. This message is part of the old check revision process. Functionality is now incorporated into C>S 0x50 SID_AUTH_INFO.
Sell an item to a Non Player Character.
Requests a list of channels that the client is permitted to enter.
Contains the return value from MCP Startup.
Response to a clan remove member request.
Contains the response to the XSHA-1 logon request from C>S 0x3A SID_LOGONRESPONSE2.
This message is sent to the server after a successful SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTLOGON. It contains the client's password proof. See [NLS/SRP Protocol] for more information.
This message reports success or failure for a password change operation.
Uses Left skill on specified (X,Y) map coordinate.
Updated C>S 0x05 SID_CLIENTID, sends client identification numbers.
This packet requests the filetime of the specified file. The second field appears to always be null.
This message contains the hashed data.

Sent to clients supporting this packet when the friend-listed account's Status or Location id changes. Also sent if the client requests it with [SID_FRIENDSUPDATE]. The first friend is Entry number 0.
Alert specifying server revision.
Retrieves a list of games.
This packet is used to keep firewalls happy and improve NAT support. It should be sent every 3-5 minutes.
Battle.net requests data from the Windows Registry using this message. This message is no longer used by Battle.net.
Uses the specified item in the player's belt.
Contains the rank of the user specified by the request.

Removes an item from the stack Please note: This message's official name is not known, and has been invented. The contents of this packet may be incomplete or incorrect, and your comments and contributions are requested.
Notifies you when your rank has been changed.
This packet is used for multiple purposes on Warcraft III. Known and validated purposes are listed here.
This packet's use is currently unconfirmed.

This message will give you data you need for SID_AUTH_ACCOUNTUPGRADE (0x57).
This message is used to upgrade an existing account from Old Logon System to New Logon System.
You must send this before you can send BNLS_UPGRADEPROOF (0x08).
If the bot ID is not recognized by the server, then this message is still sent as backwards compatibility with the previous version of BNLS, which required authorization.
This message is no longer used; developers should use the S>C 0x3C SID_CHECKDATAFILE2 message.
Requests the clan's message of the day.
Makes your character run to the Entity specified in Entity ID.
Request and manipulate database data.
Send cycle request (defunct).
Response to a clan disband request.
Change the database's password.
The slot index is returned since individual operations may be returned in a different order than they are requested.
Requests for Battle.net to reset an account's password. This message must be sent before logging in.
Enables UDP support.
Reports success or failure on a password change operation.

Sends the results from the CheckRevision() operation as well as CD key information to the server for verification. Parameters for this operation are returned in SID_AUTH_INFO. For more information on this operation, see the CheckRevision document.
Requests a profile for a user in the WarCraft III profile system.
Moves item from player's cursor buffer into Horadric cube.
Deletion response.
Reports success/failure on version challenge.
This message logs on to the BNLS server.
This message is used to switch from W3XP to WAR3.

This packet is received after successfully logging onto Battle.net and usually after receiving the first initial chat events. If the client does not respond to this packet, the client gets dropped two minutes later (give or take about 10 seconds).
Uses your left skill on the Entity specefied in Entity ID, while holding the hotkey for standing still(shift).
Uses your left skill on the Entity specefied in Entity ID, while holding the hotkey for standing still(shift).
Uses your right skill on the Entity specefied in Entity ID, while holding the hotkey for standing still(shift).
Uses your right skill on the Entity specefied in Entity ID, while holding the hotkey for standing still(shift).
Pickup an item from a Body Location to you're cursor buffer.
Sent after a successful logon to an account in a WCG tournament.
I'm sure there are many others by looking at the gaps here.

The packet is encrypted via standard RC4 hashing, using one key for outbound data and another for inbound. Its purpose is to return executable data and checksum information from various Warden modules. Full information on how to handle this packet may be found at the Rudimentary Warden information topic.
Converts the stored XSHA-1 password hash to a new NLS/SRP password. However, this message is no longer responded to, and upgrading accounts is therefore impossible.
When the game crashes, (usually) a file named Crashdump is created. If this file exists at the next logon, the contents of it are sent to Battle.net in this message.
This is sent to the entire local area network to detect games.
This is sent every 5 seconds over a UDP broadcast to update the local area network about the game.
Notifies the local area network that a game was created.
Notifies the local area network about a game (occurs every 5 seconds or refresh slots).
Notifies the local area network that a game is no longer being hosted.
A client sends this to the game host to execute an action in-game.
Informs the client about an action in-game.
This is sent from the game host to a client that just joined to check if the client has the map.
This tells the client that it is now in the downloading state and should expect chunks of file data.
A client sends this to the host to initiate a map download.
This is received when you are downloading a map from the host.
The client sends this when it has successfully received a chunk of the map file from the host client.
A client sends this to the host to enter the game lobby.
This is sent by the host every 30 seconds to make sure that the Client is still responsive. The Client should respond with C>S 0x46 W3GS_PONG_TO_HOST.
This is sent in response to S>C 0x01 W3GS_HOSTECHOREQ.
Client requests an echo from another client (occurs every 10 seconds).
This is sent in response to an echo from another client.
Tells a client about a player's information.
A client sends this to another client to gain information about self when connected.
A client requests to leave.
This is the response to C>S 0x21 W3GS_LEAVEREQ.
This is received from the game host when a player leaves.
This is sent in a response to a request to join the game lobby and indicates that the request was denied.
Sent to all other clients in-game to notify that a player has finished loading.
The game has begun the countdown to start.
The game has finished the countdown and has now started. Players should see a loading screen for the map once this is received.
This is sent to tell the client about the game slots, upon entry of the lobby.
This is sent for slot updates.
This is sent from the host to each Client to print a message on the screen from a player.
The client sends this to the host when they have finished loading the map.
This is sent to the host from each client.
This is sent from the client to the host to send a message to the other clients.
This is a reply to a client's request for games.
This is sent from the client to tell the host about the map file on the client's local system.
Informs the client about an action in-game.
More research is required.
Warns and disconnects the Client with a custom message from BNLS.
This packet can currently support 2 methods for SC/BW/W3ROC/W3TFT.

Provides the Client with the proper response to a S>C 0x5E SID_WARDEN message. If the Client receives result 0x00, then the Client should respond to Battle.net with the Data in C>S 0x5E SID_WARDEN, otherwise the Client should not respond to the message.
Rudimentary hack detection system. Was never used probably due to terrible implementation with little security.
Rudimentary hack detection system. Was never used probably due to terrible implementation with little security.

Used to send announcements and arbitrary messages to the user, but this was soon superseded by S>C 0x0F SID_CHATEVENT subcommands such as EID_INFO, EID_ERROR, and EID_BROADCAST. Printed out with the same color and style as an EID_BROADCAST.
Much like a website cookie, simply stores some arbitrary string to a 'cookie jar' to save preferences et al. which can be retrieved later by the server.
Much like a website cookie, simply stores some arbitrary string to a 'cookie jar' to save preferences et al. which can be retrieved later by the server.

Unknown. The person who found this was unable to disassemble Warcraft III's game.dll without a lot of trouble, and therefore had limited knowledge of it. It has been seen once, after SID_LOGONPROOF (0x54) in the NLS logon sequence.

This packet is used to find a Diablo II character in the ladder. Hardcore and Expansion can either be 1 or 0 and define the ladder type (always Overall). The server responds with MCP_REQUESTLADDERDATA, listing (and possibly highlighting using character flag 0x08?) the page of ranks in which the character is found.

This message is sent by Diablo II in response to ExtraWork and is thought to be part of cheat detection. This message is largely undocumented and more research is needed.
